title: if let Err(e) = Error::check(rc) cleanup pattern should use inspect_err
description: key.rs:76-83 and 101-107 use 'if let Err(e) = Error::check(rc) { flush_srk; return Err(e); }'. If MSRV >= 1.76, idiomatic Rust expresses this as Error::check(rc).inspect_err(|_| flush_srk())?. If MSRV < 1.76, use .map_err(|e| { flush; e })?. The current form is not wrong but the ? operator with a combinator is the standard pattern. Note: wolftpm has no declared rust-version — the workspace MSRV is 1.71, so map_err is the safe suggestion.
